title: Sati translator-drift: memory→mindfulness at the English-translator boundary

hypotheses.h1: H1 (house thesis — drift is at the TRANSLATOR boundary, not the Pali): For sati-stem passages, the English gloss assigned by a translator shifts systematically with translator vintage/lineage. Older / more philologically-oriented translators (nanamoli, walshe, piyadassi, PED-aligned ati-corpus renderings) retain a non-trivial share of memory/recollection glosses ('recollection', 'remembering', 'mindful recollection'), whereas newer translators (sujato/sc, thanissaro, bodhi-extract) collapse sati toward 'mindfulness' / 'awareness' as a near-monosemous default. Concretely: (a) the memory-recollection gloss-share for the oldest translator stratum exceeds that of the newest by a pre-committed margin (predict >= 5 percentage-point absolute gap on the coded gold set, direction older>newer); (b) this gap holds WITHIN matched source-strata (same set of sati passages), so it is a property of the translator, not of which texts each happened to render; (c) DPD/PED lexicographic sense-ordering lists memory/recollection as a primary or co-primary sense (lexicographic baseline that the newest translators under-realize relative to the dictionaries).
hypotheses.h0: H0 (pre-committed null, reported if it wins): There is NO systematic translator-vintage drift in sati glossing. Either (i) all translators render sati predominantly as 'mindfulness'/'awareness' with memory-recollection gloss-share < 5% across ALL translator strata and no monotone vintage trend (i.e. modern consensus is universal and old translators already used 'mindfulness'), OR (ii) gloss-share differences between translators are present but track which PASSAGES each translator covers (between-translator variance vanishes once restricted to the matched common-passage set) rather than the translator's own lexical policy. Under H0 the memory→mindfulness narrative is a folk-etymology artifact, not a measurable diachronic translation shift in this corpus.

frozen_lexica.name: SATI_STEM
frozen_lexica.role: PRIMARY subject term — the passages whose translator renderings are classified. Pali-side anchor only (English gloss is read from translations table, not matched here).
frozen_lexica.notes: Anchors sati-stem passages. Includes feminine-noun declension (sati/satiṃ/satiyā), the adjectival satimant/satimā 'mindful', the PP-derived adverbial sata/sato/satā, and the high-frequency compounds satipaṭṭhāna, satindriya, satibala, sati-sambojjhaṅga, sammā-/micchā-sati, upaṭṭhita-sati. CST-vs-SC: ṭṭh~tth, ṅg~ng, ā~a in sandhi forms — both spellings included. paṭissati/patissati = 'remembrance' (the explicitly-memory cousin) included as it co-occurs in the sati gloss cluster. appamāda (heedfulness) is the borderline neighbour, kept for collocation pass but NOT for the gold-set draw (see scope). DANGER: ṭ ṭh ṅ ñ ā ī ū ṃ must be the real diacritics — a wrong-diacritic regex silently returns zero.

frozen_lexica.name: MEMORY_NATIVE
frozen_lexica.role: DEMOTED descriptive supplement — native Pali memory-lexeme density. Do NOT use as convergent evidence for the translator drift claim (true-by-construction: these ARE memory words).
frozen_lexica.notes: Native memory verbs/nouns: sarati 'remembers' + derivations (saraṇa as 'remembrance', distinct from saraṇa 'refuge' — POLYSEMY, must be sense-coded if surfaced), anussarati/anussati 'recollection', paṭissarati 'recall'. Negative-memory: muṭṭhassati/muṭṭhasati 'muddled/lost mindfulness', sammosa/sammussati 'confusion/forgetting', asammosa 'non-confusion' (CAUTION: asammosa vs asammoha 'non-delusion' are routinely confused/conflated across editions — BOTH spellings included so the regex does not silently drop the variant edition uses). apilāpana(tā) = the canonical Vism/commentarial gloss of sati ('non-floating', steadiness) — load-bearing for the lexicographic-baseline pass. smṛti included for any Sanskrit-parallel intrusions. THIS LEXICON IS DESCRIPTIVE ONLY.

frozen_lexica.name: GLOSS_MEMORY_EN
frozen_lexica.role: English classifier lexicon, class = MEMORY-RECOLLECTION. Deterministic lexical match against translations.text for the coding pass + auto-pre-label.
frozen_lexica.pali_regex: (?i)\m(recollect|recollection|recollecting|remember|remembering|remembrance|remembered|recall|recalling|call to mind|calling to mind|bear in mind|bearing in mind|keep in mind|memory|mnemonic|retentive)
frozen_lexica.notes: NB this is an ENGLISH regex applied to translations.text, not Pali — diacritics N/A. Matches the memory/recollection gloss family. 'bear/keep in mind' is the ambiguous bridge phrase (memory-flavoured but used for mindfulness too) — flagged for human κ, not auto-final.

frozen_lexica.name: GLOSS_MINDFUL_EN
frozen_lexica.role: English classifier lexicon, class = MINDFULNESS. Deterministic lexical match against translations.text.
frozen_lexica.notes: ENGLISH regex on translations.text. The modern-consensus class. heedful(ness) is normally appamāda's gloss but some translators use it for sati — kept here, disambiguated against the appamāda Pali anchor in coding.

frozen_lexica.name: GLOSS_AWARE_EN
frozen_lexica.role: English classifier lexicon, class = AWARENESS-ATTENTION (the third bucket, distinct from both memory and mindfulness).
frozen_lexica.notes: ENGLISH regex on translations.text. 'alert/alertness' is thanissaro's frequent rendering of sampajañña not sati — must be human-checked when it co-fires with a sati anchor; kept as AWARENESS bucket. Anything matching none of MEMORY/MINDFUL/AWARE = class OTHER (the 4th bucket), resolved by embedding tie-break + human.

enumeration_sql.label: 00_translator_inventory — per-translator passage coverage & char volume (defines the matched-passage universe and the per-translator denominators; H1(b) needs the common set)
enumeration_sql.sql: SELECT t.translator, count(*) AS n_renderings, count(DISTINCT t.passage_id) AS n_passages, sum(char_length(t.text)) AS en_chars FROM translations t JOIN passages p ON p.id=t.passage_id WHERE p.is_primary AND p.original ~* '\m(sati|satiṃ|satiyā|satimant|satimā|sata|sato|satipaṭṭhān|satipatthān|satisambojjhaṅg|satindriy|satibal|sammāsati|micchāsati|upaṭṭhitasati|upatthitasati|paṭissati|patissati)' GROUP BY t.translator ORDER BY n_passages DESC;

enumeration_sql.label: 01_sati_passage_universe — every sati-stem CANON passage (dedup'd), with stratum + work_role, that has >=1 English rendering (the population the gold set is drawn from)
enumeration_sql.sql: SELECT p.id, p.work_slug, p.work_role, stratum(p.work_slug) AS strat, char_length(p.original) AS pali_chars, count(t.translator) AS n_translators FROM passages p JOIN translations t ON t.passage_id=p.id WHERE p.is_primary AND p.original ~* '\m(sati|satiṃ|satiyā|satimant|satimā|sata|sato|satipaṭṭhān|satipatthān|satisambojjhaṅg|satindriy|satibal|sammāsati|micchāsati|upaṭṭhitasati|upatthitasati|paṭissati|patissati)' GROUP BY p.id,p.work_slug,p.work_role ORDER BY strat, p.work_slug, p.id;

enumeration_sql.label: 02_pali_memory_native_density — DEMOTED descriptive supplement: native memory-lexeme hits per Mchar by layer (canon vs commentary). Reported, NOT convergent evidence.
enumeration_sql.sql: WITH den(role,mchar) AS (VALUES ('mula',53.543921),('attha',29.488533),('tika',28.357746)) SELECT CASE WHEN p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'mula' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR p.work_slug='pli-vism' THEN 'attha' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'tika' ELSE 'other' END AS layer, count(*) FILTER (WHERE p.original ~* '\m(sarati|anussarati|anussati|paṭissarati|patissarati|muṭṭhassati|muṭṭhasati|sammussati|sammosa|asammosa|asammoha|pamuṭṭh|apilāpana)') AS memory_hits, d.mchar, round(count(*) FILTER (WHERE p.original ~* '\m(sarati|anussarati|anussati|paṭissarati|patissarati|muṭṭhassati|muṭṭhasati|sammussati|sammosa|asammosa|asammoha|pamuṭṭh|apilāpana)')/d.mchar,2) AS per_mchar FROM passages p JOIN den d ON d.role=(CASE WHEN p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'mula' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR p.work_slug='pli-vism' THEN 'attha' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'tika' ELSE 'x' END) WHERE p.is_primary GROUP BY layer,d.mchar ORDER BY layer;

enumeration_sql.label: 03_gloss_autolabel_per_translator — deterministic lexical pre-label of every sati-passage rendering into MEMORY/MINDFUL/AWARE/OTHER, counted per translator (the raw gloss-share matrix; OTHER + multi-class rows go to embedding tie-break + human)
enumeration_sql.sql: SELECT t.translator, count(*) AS n, count(*) FILTER (WHERE t.text ~* '(?i)\m(recollect|remember|remembrance|recall|call to mind|bear in mind|keep in mind|memory|retentive)') AS memory, count(*) FILTER (WHERE t.text ~* '(?i)\m(mindful|heedful)') AS mindful, count(*) FILTER (WHERE t.text ~* '(?i)\m(aware|attention|attentive|alert|present|wakeful|watchful|vigilan|circumspect)') AS aware FROM translations t JOIN passages p ON p.id=t.passage_id WHERE p.is_primary AND p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.original ~* '\m(sati|satiṃ|satiyā|satimant|satimā|sata|sato|satipaṭṭhān|satipatthān|satisambojjhaṅg|satindriy|satibal|sammāsati|micchāsati|upaṭṭhitasati|upatthitasati|paṭissati|patissati)' GROUP BY t.translator ORDER BY n DESC;

enumeration_sql.label: 04_gloss_share_by_source_stratum — same auto-label but split by SOURCE stratum (per translator x stratum), to separate translator-policy drift (H1) from passage-mix confound (H0-ii)
enumeration_sql.sql: SELECT t.translator, stratum(p.work_slug) AS strat, count(*) AS n, count(*) FILTER (WHERE t.text ~* '(?i)\m(recollect|remember|remembrance|recall|call to mind|bear in mind|keep in mind|memory|retentive)') AS memory, count(*) FILTER (WHERE t.text ~* '(?i)\m(mindful|heedful)') AS mindful FROM translations t JOIN passages p ON p.id=t.passage_id WHERE p.is_primary AND p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.original ~* '\m(sati|satiṃ|satiyā|satimant|satimā|sata|sato|satipaṭṭhān|satipatthān|satisambojjhaṅg|satindriy|satibal|sammāsati|micchāsati|upaṭṭhitasati|upatthitasati|paṭissati|patissati)' GROUP BY t.translator, strat ORDER BY t.translator, strat;

enumeration_sql.label: 05_matched_common_passage_set — sati passages rendered by >=3 translators incl at least one 'old' (nanamoli/walshe/piyadassi) AND one 'new' (sujato/thanissaro/bodhi-extract); this is the WITHIN-passage drift test (H1b) that neutralizes passage-mix
enumeration_sql.sql: SELECT p.id, p.work_slug, stratum(p.work_slug) AS strat, array_agg(t.translator ORDER BY t.translator) AS translators FROM passages p JOIN translations t ON t.passage_id=p.id WHERE p.is_primary AND p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.original ~* '\m(sati|satiṃ|satiyā|satimant|satimā|sata|sato|satipaṭṭhān|satipatthān|satisambojjhaṅg|satindriy|satibal|sammāsati|micchāsati|upaṭṭhitasati|upatthitasati|paṭissati|patissati)' GROUP BY p.id,p.work_slug HAVING count(DISTINCT t.translator) FILTER (WHERE t.translator IN ('nanamoli','walshe','piyadassi')) >= 1 AND count(DISTINCT t.translator) FILTER (WHERE t.translator IN ('sujato','thanissaro','bodhi-extract')) >= 1 ORDER BY strat, p.id;

enumeration_sql.label: 06_gold_rows_to_code — the rows handed to blind κ coders: passage_id + translator + Pali snippet + the rendered English snippet, drawn from the matched set (05) PLUS a primary-loci forced-include, capped per translator for balance
enumeration_sql.sql: SELECT p.id AS passage_id, t.translator, stratum(p.work_slug) AS strat, p.work_slug, left(p.original,400) AS pali_snippet, left(t.text,400) AS en_snippet FROM translations t JOIN passages p ON p.id=t.passage_id WHERE p.is_primary AND p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.original ~* '\m(sati|satiṃ|satiyā|satimant|satimā|sata|sato|satipaṭṭhān|satipatthān|satisambojjhaṅg|satindriy|satibal|sammāsati|micchāsati|upaṭṭhitasati|upatthitasati|paṭissati|patissati)' AND (p.work_slug IN ('dn-22','mn-10','mn-118','sn-47','an-1') OR t.translator IN ('nanamoli','walshe','piyadassi','sujato','thanissaro','bodhi-extract','piyadassi','bodhi')) ORDER BY t.translator, strat, p.id LIMIT 1200;

enumeration_sql.label: 07_lexicographic_baseline_dpd_ped — DPD/PED/Monier sense-ordering for sati & anussati: does the dictionary list memory/recollection as primary/co-primary? (the lexicographic baseline H1c compares newest translators against)
enumeration_sql.sql: SELECT d.source, d.headword, left(d.definition, 600) AS sense_order FROM dictionary_entries d WHERE d.headword ~* '\m(sati|anussati|sarati|paṭissati|apilāpana)' AND d.source IN ('DPD','PED','Monier-Williams') ORDER BY d.source, d.headword;

enumeration_sql.label: 08_NEGCTRL_matched_nonsati_term — NEGATIVE CONTROL: a non-sati high-frequency canonical term (paññā 'wisdom') of comparable register/length must NOT show translator-vintage gloss divergence. Same auto-label machinery, per translator.
enumeration_sql.sql: SELECT t.translator, count(*) AS n, count(*) FILTER (WHERE t.text ~* '(?i)\m(wisdom|wise|discernment|understanding|insight|sagacit)') AS wisdom_cluster, count(*) FILTER (WHERE t.text ~* '(?i)\m(recollect|remember|memory|mindful|aware)') AS drift_cluster FROM translations t JOIN passages p ON p.id=t.passage_id WHERE p.is_primary AND p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.original ~* '\m(paññā|paññāya|paññāṇ|pañña|paññav|paññāpet)' GROUP BY t.translator ORDER BY n DESC;

enumeration_sql.label: 09_NEGCTRL_within_passage_stability — NEGATIVE CONTROL within the matched-passage frame: for the same paññā passages rendered by old+new translators, gloss concordance should be HIGH (translators agree), proving the sati divergence is term-specific not generic register
enumeration_sql.sql: SELECT p.id, count(DISTINCT t.translator) AS n_tr, count(*) FILTER (WHERE t.text ~* '(?i)\m(wisdom|discernment|understanding|insight)') AS wisdom_glosses FROM passages p JOIN translations t ON t.passage_id=p.id WHERE p.is_primary AND p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.original ~* '\m(paññā|paññāya|paññāṇ|paññav)' GROUP BY p.id HAVING count(DISTINCT t.translator) FILTER (WHERE t.translator IN ('nanamoli','walshe','piyadassi')) >= 1 AND count(DISTINCT t.translator) FILTER (WHERE t.translator IN ('sujato','thanissaro','bodhi-extract')) >= 1 ORDER BY p.id;

enumeration_sql.label: 10_collocation_sati_with_memory_pali — descriptive collocation: of sati-stem passages, how many ALSO carry a native memory lexeme on the Pali side (context that may license a memory gloss); per stratum, per Mchar-normalized count
enumeration_sql.sql: SELECT stratum(p.work_slug) AS strat, count(*) AS sati_passages, count(*) FILTER (WHERE p.original ~* '\m(sarati|anussarati|anussati|paṭissarati|patissarati|saraṇ)') AS also_memory_pali, round(100.0*count(*) FILTER (WHERE p.original ~* '\m(sarati|anussarati|anussati|paṭissarati|patissarati|saraṇ)')/nullif(count(*),0),1) AS pct FROM passages p WHERE p.is_primary AND p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.original ~* '\m(sati|satiṃ|satiyā|satimant|satimā|sata|sato|satipaṭṭhān|satisambojjhaṅg|satindriy|paṭissati|patissati)' GROUP BY strat ORDER BY strat;

density_method: All Pali-side magnitudes are per-character, never raw rows. Native memory-lexeme density (query 02) divides FILTERed hit-counts by the frozen per-layer Mchar denominators (mula 53.543921, attha 29.488533, tika 28.357746) and is reported as hits/Mchar by layer; this is DESCRIPTIVE SUPPLEMENT ONLY and explicitly excluded from convergent-evidence claims (memory lexemes ARE memory words — predicting stratum density would be true by construction). The PRIMARY measure is NOT a density: it is the per-translator gloss-SHARE — for each translator, the fraction of their sati-stem renderings auto-labelled (then human-confirmed) MEMORY-RECOLLECTION / MINDFULNESS / AWARENESS / OTHER. Denominators are per-translator rendering counts (query 00). The drift statistic is the absolute percentage-point gap in MEMORY-share between the oldest and newest translator strata, computed TWICE: (a) over each translator's full sati corpus (query 03), and (b) over the matched common-passage set rendered by both an old and a new translator (queries 05/06) to neutralize the passage-mix confound. Significance by a passage-clustered bootstrap (resample passages, not renderings) over the human-coded gold set; report the gap with a 90% interval. Stratum() is used only to slice source provenance of passages, never as independent chronology (T1).
negative_controls: paññā ('wisdom') matched-register control (query 08): a non-sati high-frequency abstract canonical term must NOT show a translator-vintage gloss split. If old vs new translators diverge on paññā as much as on sati, the sati signal is a generic register/length/translator-idiolect artifact, not term-specific drift — claim fails.

Within-matched-passage concordance control (query 09): on the SAME paññā passages rendered by both old and new translators, gloss concordance must be HIGH. High paññā concordance + low sati concordance on structurally identical passage frames is the clean discriminator that isolates the effect to sati.

Auto-label vs human-code agreement control: the deterministic lexical pre-label must reach the pre-set κ floor against blind human coders on the gold set; if the lexical matcher disagrees materially with humans (κ below floor), the auto-label gloss-shares (03/04) are discarded and only human-coded shares are reported.

Sense-split control on saraṇa: saraṇa appears as both 'remembrance' (memory) and 'refuge' — query 02's memory count must be re-audited so refuge-sense saraṇa is not silently inflating native memory density (the %carita% lesson).
falsifiers: Memory-recollection gloss-share is < 5% for EVERY translator stratum (including the oldest) AND shows no monotone vintage trend → H1 falsified, H0(i) reported: modern 'mindfulness' consensus is universal, no measurable drift.

The old-minus-new MEMORY-share gap that appears in the full-corpus pass (03) collapses to within the bootstrap interval once restricted to the matched common-passage set (05/06) → H1(b) falsified, H0(ii) reported: the apparent drift was a passage-mix confound, not translator policy.
The negative control paññā shows an old-vs-new gloss split of comparable magnitude to sati → the effect is a generic translator-idiolect/register artifact, sati-specific drift claim withdrawn.

Inter-annotator κ on the 4-way gloss coding falls below the pre-set floor (0.60) and cannot be repaired by codebook revision on a held-out batch → the gloss classes are not reliably distinguishable; no share comparison is reportable.

DPD/PED sense-ordering does NOT list memory/recollection as primary or co-primary for sati/anussati (query 07) → the lexicographic-baseline leg H1(c) is withdrawn (the dictionaries themselves lead with mindfulness/awareness).
predicted_signal: PREDICTED (to be confirmed): oldest translator stratum (nanamoli / walshe / piyadassi-era) MEMORY-recollection gloss-share materially above newest (sujato / thanissaro / bodhi-extract), absolute gap predicted >= 5 percentage points, direction old>new, surviving the matched-passage restriction (05/06). PREDICTED: paññā negative control shows old~new agreement (gap < 2pp) and high within-passage concordance. PREDICTED (lexicographic): DPD/PED list recollection/memory as a primary-or-co-primary sense of sati & anussati, i.e. newest translators under-realize the dictionary's memory sense. TO BE MEASURED (no prediction registered): the absolute memory-share level for any translator; the native-memory hits/Mchar by layer (query 02, descriptive only); the share of sati passages that also carry a native memory lexeme on the Pali side (query 10); the size of the OTHER/AWARENESS buckets; the exact set membership of the matched common-passage universe. The mapping of specific translator names to old/new strata is fixed in advance (old = {nanamoli, walshe, piyadassi}; new = {sujato, thanissaro, bodhi-extract}); bps/other translators are coded but not assigned to a vintage pole.
primary_loci: dn-22 (Mahāsatipaṭṭhāna Sutta) — the foundational sati locus; forced-include in gold set
mn-118 (Ānāpānasati Sutta) — sati as the bridge between breath-watching and the bojjhaṅgas
sn-48 / satindriya passages (sati as a faculty/power, where 'recollection' reading is strongest)
an-1 and the anussati / cha-anussati series (recollection of Buddha/Dhamma/Saṅgha/sīla/cāga/devatā) — the locus where the memory sense is least deniable
pli-vism (Visuddhimagga) sati definition via apilāpana — the commentarial gloss, used for the lexicographic/commentarial baseline only (commentary, not canon numerator)
scope_limits: Translator-boundary study, not a Pali-semantics study: it measures English rendering policy, not what sati 'really means'. A null says nothing about the Pali sense of sati.

'Vintage' is operationalized by translator identity, not verified publication date per passage; our corpus has no per-rendering year, so old/new is a fixed name-to-pole assignment, a coarse proxy for true diachrony (analogous to the T1 stratum-is-lookup caution).

Coverage is uneven: translators do not render the same passages; the full-corpus pass (03) is confounded by passage mix, which is exactly why the matched-set pass (05/06) is the decisive test and the full-corpus pass is supporting only.
No foreign-language parallel text is held (parallel_have=false for non-pli); any Chinese/Sanskrit gloss comparison is impossible — graph-only.
Cannot settle WHY drift happens (Burmese vipassanā-movement influence, dictionary lineage, target-audience register) — it can only show that the rendering shifts, not its cause.

appamāda (heedfulness) and sampajañña (clear-comprehension) neighbours can leak into the AWARENESS/MINDFULNESS English buckets; coding mitigates but cannot fully separate translator conflation of these near-terms from sati proper.
Native memory-density (02, 10) is descriptive supplement; it is structurally barred from corroborating the drift thesis and must not be reported as convergent evidence.
iaa_plan: Two blind coders (one human operator + one independent agent coder) independently 4-way code the gold rows from query 06 (passage_id + translator + Pali snippet + English snippet ONLY; translator-name and vintage HIDDEN/randomized to prevent expectancy bias) into MEMORY-RECOLLECTION / MINDFULNESS / AWARENESS / OTHER, against a frozen codebook with the GLOSS_*_EN lexica as decision aids and explicit tie-break rules (e.g. 'bear/keep in mind' → MEMORY only if a remember/recall token co-occurs, else AWARENESS; 'alert/alertness' → AWARENESS, flag if sampajañña anchor present). Target n >= 300 coded rows balanced across translator and stratum (cap per translator from query 06). Compute Cohen's κ on the 4-way scheme; pre-set floor κ >= 0.60 to proceed, with a target of 0.70. Disagreements adjudicated by a third pass; if κ < 0.60, revise codebook ONCE on a held-out calibration batch (separate passage ids) and re-code — if still below floor, the gloss comparison is declared unreportable (falsifier 4). The deterministic auto-label (queries 03/04) is validated against the consensus human codes; auto-shares are reported only if auto-vs-human κ also clears 0.60, otherwise human-coded shares are the sole basis.
